heaviest_rainfall.status: Record amounts of rain fell over 125.7 hours today with a total accumulation of 42.26mm.  It fell at 2.2 mm per hour at times and ranked top from our records.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 27.0mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: Today's rainstorm ranked highest on our record table for the length of the storm as it lasted 125.7 hours; it delivered 42.26mm of rain at a maximum rate of 2.2mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: It is currently one of the longest rainy periods.  It is the ranked 9th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 8 consecutive days where it has been rainy starting on 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (186°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%) and rainfall (up 0.2 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 342 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 430 m), rainfall (down 0.2 mm), feels like temperature (down 1.9°C), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 342.1m lower than yesterday, 206.6m lower than the past week, 430.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 637m and a high of 1658m, averaging 611.3m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air moisture should feel ok and mostly calm with light air movement. A few light raindrops may fall, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and dim or diffuse light conditions. Changeable conditions.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
